task_name: N/A - Not an FLS Task Video
explanation: This video is an educational presentation from SAGES 2014 (Surgical Spring Week) focused on operating room teamwork, communication, and simulation-based training. It contains lecture slides, narration about team dynamics in the OR, case study examples, and educational content about high-reliability teams. It does NOT contain any FLS/MISTELS task performance (Peg Transfer, Pattern Cut, Endoloop, Extracorporeal Suture, or Intracorporeal Suture). The frames show presentation slides, conference speakers, operating room team photos, and conceptual diagrams—not laparoscopic task execution.
reason_for_non_scoring: This is purely instructional/lecture content with no trainee or expert performance of an FLS task visible. No laparoscopic instruments, trainer box, or task-specific equipment (pegs, gauze, foam appendage, Penrose drain, suture) are present. The video's educational value is in teaching OR team communication and safety concepts, not in demonstrating technical laparoscopic skills.
content_summary: The presentation discusses teamwork failures in the OR, communication breakdowns, consequences of poor team function, solutions via simulation-based training, and the expansion of such programs to inter-professional students globally.

verbal_cues: Current status of teamwork in the OR is suboptimal
Surgeon-initiated communication occurs 80% of the time
High-reliability teams possess teachable knowledge, skills, and attitudes
Simulation-based OR team training improves team-based attitudes and function
common_errors_addressed: Misunderstanding of information in the OR
Disruptive behaviors: yelling, disrespect, patronizing, berating, abusive language
External interruptions and distractions (one every 4 minutes in real OR)
Lack of coordination between hospital departments
teaching_content.skill_level_demonstrated: N/A - Educational/leadership content, not technical surgical skill
teaching_content.primary_topic: Operating Room Team Training and Safety Culture
